Chris Berry Art

Lost In The Forest 17
Gargantua
2021

About This Painting

  • A hare flees through a field of foxgloves on the event horizon of a black hole.
  • Titled ‘Gargantua’ after the iconic supermassive black hole in Christopher Nolan’s, Interstellar.
  • Inspired by the wild foxgloves that grow abundantly across Dartmoor National Park, a place I love to hike.
  • A paradox of nature: foxgloves are beautiful, yet toxic to the heart…
  • The background pattern uses Penrose tiling to represent the structural fabric of reality.
  • Invented by Sir Roger Penrose, the pattern’s a nod to his parallel discoveries in geometry and black hole physics.

Available as a Fine Art Print

  • Printed on Hahnemühle Photo Rag — deep blacks, vibrant colour
  • Standard A3 (11.6 × 16.5 in) — ready for any off-the-shelf frame
  • Giclée quality: fade-resistant, gallery-standard
  • Certificate of Authenticity included
  • Free shipping within the UK
